---
title: "Part 3: Interpolation and Solver Behavior"
bibliography: ../../references.bib
---

[Back to regularizeNd series home](index.qmd)

[Previous: Part 2](part-02-1d-smoothing-and-parameter-tuning.qmd)

This part focuses on interpolation choices and solver strategy in regularizeNd.

## Practical Goal

- Compare interpolation methods: linear, nearest, cubic.
- Compare solver behavior for representative problem sizes.
- Understand when the normal-equation pathway is useful and when iterative options are needed.

## Example Anchor

Primary script: `regularizeNd/Examples/example_solver_differences.m`

## Suggested Walkthrough

1. Fix data and grid.
2. Run selected solver configurations.
3. Compare run time, stability, and output shape.
4. Keep notes on conditioning-sensitive settings.

## Key Point

Interpolation choice should be based on output behavior and accuracy needs, not only local computational complexity.

## Attribution

This educational progression is inspired by Peter Goldstein's regularization articles and related prior tools.

## Next

[Part 4: Boundary Behavior and Convex Hull](part-04-boundary-behavior-and-convex-hull.qmd)
